title: heliotropium gibberosum
intro: Heliotropium gibberosum, also known as Hairy Heliotrope or Sticky Finger Flowers, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family that is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Heliotropium gibberosum include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent to prevent infection and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall health and well-being.
